sections.title: API Access Control — Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA)

items.title: Tamper with sequential object identifiers
items.description: Increment, decrement, or substitute numeric object IDs in path and query parameters to access resources belonging to other users. If exploited, this often leads to full account or environment compromise and must be treated as a release blocker. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Authenticate as user A, request user B object IDs; a 200 with B data instead of 403/404 confirms BOLA. Common tags: api, api1-2023, authorization.

sections.title: API Resource & Posture — Unsafe Consumption of APIs

items.title: Test for blind trust of third-party API data
items.description: Data from integrated APIs consumed without validation can carry injection or malformed payloads downstream. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. items.title: Test inbound webhook signature verification
items.description: Endpoints that receive provider webhooks (payment, VCS, CI) must verify the HMAC/asymmetric signature; missing or flawed verification lets an attacker forge state-changing events. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Replay a webhook with the signature header removed, an empty/zeroed signature, a wrong algorithm, or a tampered body; confirm rejection. Test for timing-unsafe comparison and missing timestamp/replay protection on valid events. Common tags: api, api10-2023, webhook, signature-verification.
